name: Famous Legends and Lore of the North
author: Harbalor "SnowSage" Ilthon
description:
Harbalor Ilthon — called SnowSage in tavern verse — collects ballads, campfire tales, and disputed histories from Icewind Dale to the High Forest.

Each legend is presented twice: the popular version sung in inns, and the older variant SnowSage claims predates human settlement. Readers are left to decide which is true.

name: Forty Years Before the Doors
author: Zanthravas Rolovantar
description:
Forty Years Before the Doors — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Zanthravas Rolovantar is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1363 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Falcon Fun
author: Edwin Narlok of Waterdeep
description:
Falcon Fun — Natural history from hunters, riverfolk, and sages who went outside and wrote down what bit them. Edwin Narlok of Waterdeep is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1344 DR.

Seasonal addenda in surviving copies suggest the author revised the work every year they lived to see spring.

name: Fallen Legacies: Arcaeological and Historical Analysis of the Mere of Dead Men
author: Khelben "Blackstaff" Arunsun
description:
Fallen Legacies: Arcaeological and Historical Analysis of the Mere of Dead Men — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Khelben "Blackstaff" Arunsun is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1346 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Folio of Brass
author: Tellar Moendath of Luskan
description:
Folio of Brass — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Tellar Moendath of Luskan is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1250 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Folklore of Faerûn
author: Unknown
description:
Folklore of Faerûn — Legends collected from tavern verse, elder memory, and sources no scholar will swear to in court. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1372 DR.

Each generation adds a verse; purists claim the oldest layer is true and everything since is flattery.

name: Fables of Faerûn
author: Unknown
description:
Fables of Faerûn — A popular tale in print — performed in taverns before it was ever bound between covers. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.

Critics call it low art; innkeepers call it excellent for business on rainy nights.

name: Folksongs of Faerûn
author: Unknown
description:
Folksongs of Faerûn — An in-universe work listed in Candlekeep indices and frontier libraries throughout the Heartlands. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to 1492 DR.

Copies vary by scriptorium; Candlekeep treats the title as known though completeness is never guaranteed.

name: Flow of the Chionthar
author: Unknown
description:
Flow of the Chionthar — A regional survey written for travellers who cannot afford to learn geography from dead companions. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1373 DR.

Maps are absent or obsolete in most copies; readers are expected to update the text with their own ink.

name: Flora of the Dalelands
author: Unknown
description:
Flora of the Dalelands — Natural history from hunters, riverfolk, and sages who went outside and wrote down what bit them. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1442 DR.

Seasonal addenda in surviving copies suggest the author revised the work every year they lived to see spring.

name: Four Decades of Innkeeping
author: Thundaerlel Maurlatrimm
description:
Four Decades of Innkeeping — Practical notes for wayfarers — tolls, seasons, inns, and the unwritten rules of safe passage. Thundaerlel Maurlatrimm is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1336 DR.

Experienced caravans treat the counsel as suggestion only — but rookies who ignore it seldom return to disagree.

name: Fifty Years at Sea
author: Gandorra Burr
description:
Fifty Years at Sea — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Gandorra Burr is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to 1489 DR.

Later hands added marginal dates and crossed out names; historians still argue which revisions were propaganda.

name: Festivals and Festivities of Ancient Cormyr
author: Unknown
description:
Festivals and Festivities of Ancient Cormyr — A chronicle preserved in northern and coastal scriptoria — dates, names, and the sort of detail merchants pretend not to need. Library catalogues list no certain author; the work is dated to before 1492 DR.
